    The most recent date a position changed in any way

    name()
    accounts/{account_id}/positions/{position_id}
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingDrip>
    pendingDrip()
    Represents the amount of cash that has been paid to an account due to a dividend or capital gain but is due to be reinvested in the security that paid the account holder
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingOutgoingAcat>
    pendingOutgoingAcat()
    Represents the amount of an asset that is subject to a pending outgoing account transfer, but has not completed the bookkeeping phase of that account transfer
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingWithdrawal>
    pendingWithdrawal()
    Represents the amount of cash that has been requested for withdrawal but has not posted to the Ledger
    Optional<String>
    positionVersion()
    Represents a chronologically-ordered version identifier that enables efficient position state tracking and event ordering.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Settled>
    settled()
    This field refers to the quantity of assets that have completed the entire clearing and settlement cycle, where ownership of the securities has been officially transferred and payment has been fully processed.
    String
    toString()
     
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PositionTrade>
    trade()
    This field represents the total amount of an asset owned by the account including transactions that have been executed but not yet settled, commonly known as the trade date position.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Unrestricted>
    unrestricted()
    This field represents the portion of a settled position that is available for trading or withdrawal without restrictions.

    • Position

      public Position(Optional<String> accountId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AdjustedSettled> adjustedSettled, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AdjustedTrade> adjustedTrade, Optional<String> assetId, Optional<String> correspondentId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Date> date, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PositionFpsl> fpsl, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Free> free, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends LastAdjustedDate> lastAdjustedDate, Optional<String> name, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingDrip> pendingDrip, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingOutgoingAcat> pendingOutgoingAcat, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingWithdrawal> pendingWithdrawal, Optional<String> positionVersion, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Settled> settled, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PositionTrade> trade, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Unrestricted> unrestricted)

    • Position

      public Position()

  • Method Details

    • accountId

      public Optional<String> accountId()
      A globally unique identifier referencing a single account; this is the main identifier for an account used for machine-to-machine interactions

    • adjustedSettled

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AdjustedSettled> adjustedSettled()
      This field shows settled positions that have been adjusted to account for as-of transactions (transactions recorded after their actual occurrence). Unlike the settled field, which remains unchanged for historical dates when as-of transactions are recorded, the adjusted_settled field updates to reflect what the position would have been if all transactions had been recorded on their actual dates of occurrence.

    • adjustedTrade

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AdjustedTrade> adjustedTrade()
      This value reflects trade positions that have been adjusted due to the recording of transactions after their actual occurrence (as-of transactions). The key difference between this field and the trade field is that when an as-of transaction is recorded to the Ledger, the trade field will not change for historical dates, but the adjusted_trade field will update to reflect what the position would have been if the as-of transaction had been recorded on the date of its occurrence

    • assetId

      public Optional<String> assetId()
      An Apex-provided, global identifier created on a per asset bases which provides connectivity across all areas

    • correspondentId

      public Optional<String> correspondentId()
      The correspondent id associated with the account for the position

    • date

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Date> date()
      The date for which positions were calculated

    • fpsl

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PositionFpsl> fpsl()
      Represents the amount of an asset that has been loaned out via the fully paid securities lending program

    • free

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Free> free()
      Represents the amount of an asset that is available to loan by the fully paid securities lending program.

    • lastAdjustedDate

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<LastAdjustedDate> lastAdjustedDate()
      The most recent date a position changed in any way

    • name

      public Optional<String> name()
      accounts/{account_id}/positions/{position_id}

    • pendingDrip

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingDrip> pendingDrip()
      Represents the amount of cash that has been paid to an account due to a dividend or capital gain but is due to be reinvested in the security that paid the account holder

    • pendingOutgoingAcat

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingOutgoingAcat> pendingOutgoingAcat()
      Represents the amount of an asset that is subject to a pending outgoing account transfer, but has not completed the bookkeeping phase of that account transfer

    • pendingWithdrawal

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingWithdrawal> pendingWithdrawal()
      Represents the amount of cash that has been requested for withdrawal but has not posted to the Ledger

    • positionVersion

      public Optional<String> positionVersion()
      Represents a chronologically-ordered version identifier that enables efficient position state tracking and event ordering. The system guarantees that positions from earlier dates have smaller version numbers than those from later dates

    • settled

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Settled> settled()
      This field refers to the quantity of assets that have completed the entire clearing and settlement cycle, where ownership of the securities has been officially transferred and payment has been fully processed. The settled position includes all transactions that have been recorded in the Ledger with process_date, activity_date, and settle_date on or before the date specified in the response.

    • trade

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PositionTrade> trade()
      This field represents the total amount of an asset owned by the account including transactions that have been executed but not yet settled, commonly known as the trade date position. It includes all transactions recorded in the Ledger with process_date and activity_date on or before the date in the response, even those with future settle_dates. Unlike the settled position, which only includes completed settlements, the trade position provides a forward-looking view of ownership that accounts for pending settlements

    • unrestricted

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Unrestricted> unrestricted()
      This field represents the portion of a settled position that is available for trading or withdrawal without restrictions. It is calculated by subtracting positions with pending restrictions from the total settled amount (currently: settled - (pending_outgoing_acat + pending_drip + pending_withdrawal)). As new memo location categories are added to the API, Apex may update this calculation to incorporate these values. Note that the Cash and Margin systems may place additional restrictions on cash/ assets according to their business logic.

    • builder

      public static Position.Builder builder()

      A globally unique identifier referencing a single account; this is the main identifier for an account used for machine-to-machine interactions

    • withAdjustedSettled

      public Position withAdjustedSettled(AdjustedSettled adjustedSettled)
      This field shows settled positions that have been adjusted to account for as-of transactions (transactions recorded after their actual occurrence). Unlike the settled field, which remains unchanged for historical dates when as-of transactions are recorded, the adjusted_settled field updates to reflect what the position would have been if all transactions had been recorded on their actual dates of occurrence.

    • withAdjustedSettled

      public Position withAdjustedSettled(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AdjustedSettled> adjustedSettled)
      This field shows settled positions that have been adjusted to account for as-of transactions (transactions recorded after their actual occurrence). Unlike the settled field, which remains unchanged for historical dates when as-of transactions are recorded, the adjusted_settled field updates to reflect what the position would have been if all transactions had been recorded on their actual dates of occurrence.

    • withAdjustedTrade

      public Position withAdjustedTrade(AdjustedTrade adjustedTrade)
      This value reflects trade positions that have been adjusted due to the recording of transactions after their actual occurrence (as-of transactions). The key difference between this field and the trade field is that when an as-of transaction is recorded to the Ledger, the trade field will not change for historical dates, but the adjusted_trade field will update to reflect what the position would have been if the as-of transaction had been recorded on the date of its occurrence

    • withAdjustedTrade

      public Position withAdjustedTrade(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AdjustedTrade> adjustedTrade)
      This value reflects trade positions that have been adjusted due to the recording of transactions after their actual occurrence (as-of transactions). The key difference between this field and the trade field is that when an as-of transaction is recorded to the Ledger, the trade field will not change for historical dates, but the adjusted_trade field will update to reflect what the position would have been if the as-of transaction had been recorded on the date of its occurrence

    • withPositionVersion

      public Position withPositionVersion(String positionVersion)
      Represents a chronologically-ordered version identifier that enables efficient position state tracking and event ordering. The system guarantees that positions from earlier dates have smaller version numbers than those from later dates

    • withPositionVersion

      public Position withPositionVersion(Optional<String> positionVersion)
      Represents a chronologically-ordered version identifier that enables efficient position state tracking and event ordering. The system guarantees that positions from earlier dates have smaller version numbers than those from later dates

    • withSettled

      public Position withSettled(Settled settled)
      This field refers to the quantity of assets that have completed the entire clearing and settlement cycle, where ownership of the securities has been officially transferred and payment has been fully processed. The settled position includes all transactions that have been recorded in the Ledger with process_date, activity_date, and settle_date on or before the date specified in the response.

    • withSettled

      public Position withSettled(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Settled> settled)
      This field refers to the quantity of assets that have completed the entire clearing and settlement cycle, where ownership of the securities has been officially transferred and payment has been fully processed. The settled position includes all transactions that have been recorded in the Ledger with process_date, activity_date, and settle_date on or before the date specified in the response.

    • withTrade

      public Position withTrade(PositionTrade trade)
      This field represents the total amount of an asset owned by the account including transactions that have been executed but not yet settled, commonly known as the trade date position. It includes all transactions recorded in the Ledger with process_date and activity_date on or before the date in the response, even those with future settle_dates. Unlike the settled position, which only includes completed settlements, the trade position provides a forward-looking view of ownership that accounts for pending settlements

    • withTrade

      public Position withTrade(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PositionTrade> trade)
      This field represents the total amount of an asset owned by the account including transactions that have been executed but not yet settled, commonly known as the trade date position. It includes all transactions recorded in the Ledger with process_date and activity_date on or before the date in the response, even those with future settle_dates. Unlike the settled position, which only includes completed settlements, the trade position provides a forward-looking view of ownership that accounts for pending settlements

    • withUnrestricted

      public Position withUnrestricted(Unrestricted unrestricted)
      This field represents the portion of a settled position that is available for trading or withdrawal without restrictions. It is calculated by subtracting positions with pending restrictions from the total settled amount (currently: settled - (pending_outgoing_acat + pending_drip + pending_withdrawal)). As new memo location categories are added to the API, Apex may update this calculation to incorporate these values. Note that the Cash and Margin systems may place additional restrictions on cash/ assets according to their business logic.

    • withUnrestricted

      public Position withUnrestricted(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Unrestricted> unrestricted)
      This field represents the portion of a settled position that is available for trading or withdrawal without restrictions. It is calculated by subtracting positions with pending restrictions from the total settled amount (currently: settled - (pending_outgoing_acat + pending_drip + pending_withdrawal)). As new memo location categories are added to the API, Apex may update this calculation to incorporate these values. Note that the Cash and Margin systems may place additional restrictions on cash/ assets according to their business logic.
